steps.title: Identify the attribution problem the researchers concede
steps.content: The study found tourism increases household income at both PAs. But the passage draws a key contrast: Lower Zambezi is a remote terrestrial PA with "few alternative amenities," while Abrolhos is a marine PA "close to additional amenities not part of the PA." This creates an attribution problem: at Lower Zambezi the PA itself must be drawing tourists, but at Abrolhos the nearby amenities (not the PA) may be what attracts tourists and drives income. The concession must acknowledge this distinction.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the establishment of both PAs likely benefited their respective local economies, such gains likely derived mainly from the tourism industry in Lower Zambezi and from industries unrelated to tourism in Abrolhos.': The passage says tourism drives income at Abrolhos; saying Abrolhos gains come from non-tourism industries contradicts the study's own findings. ✗
  • Choice B — 'tourism at Abrolhos and surrounding regions likely fluctuated erratically in the period shortly after the PA was created, tourism at Lower Zambezi remained largely stable.': The passage says nothing about fluctuations or stability over time; this introduces information not in the text. ✗
  • Choice C — 'economic activity in the tourism sector in communities around Lower Zambezi can likely be attributed to interest in it as a protected area, economic activity in tourism around Abrolhos may be unrelated to the PA.': This directly captures the concession: Lower Zambezi's tourism is attributable to the PA (it's the only draw), while Abrolhos's tourism income may stem from nearby amenities rather than the PA. ✓
  • Choice D — 'household income increased in the areas surrounding both PAs, it likely grew at a much faster rate for households near Abrolhos than for households near Lower Zambezi.': The passage makes no comparison of growth rates between the two PAs; this is unsupported. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
